Всеки профил, запазен тук, се появява и в падащото меню за модели в „Бърза настройка“ по-горе. Използвайте това, за да фиксирате модел с конкретни настройки за температура / токени / усилие на разсъждение, или за да изпробвате модел, който не е във вградения каталог.

projectAi.run_gate_ok: Този проект е напълно конфигуриран за AI изпълнения. Започнете с преглед и след това преминете към изпълнение на живо при нужда.
projectAi.writes_forced_preview: Записите са принудени в режим преглед
projectAi.runtime_policy_desc: Пренастройките на проекта могат допълнително да ограничат това, което работното пространство разрешава.
projectAi.enable_agents_desc: Включва изпълненията на агенти за този проект.
projectAi.inherit_defaults: Наследи настройките по подразбиране на работното пространство
projectAi.inherit_defaults_desc: Дръж доставчика, режима на изпълнение и възможностите в съответствие с политиката на работното пространство.
projectAi.allow_writes_desc: Необходимо за редакции на бек лога и създаване на партиди спринтове.
projectAi.auto_assign_desc: Поставяй задачите в планирани спринтове при създаване на партиди.
projectAi.effective_controls_desc: Окончателната политика, след като се приложат предпазните мерки на работното пространство и администратора.
projectAi.blocking_items_note: {count, plural, one {# блокиращ елемент за настройка} other {# блокиращи елемента за настройка}} трябва да бъдат поправени, преди да може да започне изпълнение.
projectAi.policy_notes_note: {count, plural, one {# бележка за политиката} other {# бележки за политиката}} все още влияят на поведението при писане и автономността.
projectAi.service_status_desc: Конкретни бариери за изпълнение за този проект.
projectAi.capabilities_desc: Всяка възможност може да бъде стеснена допълнително спрямо стойността по подразбиране на работното пространство.
projectAi.manual_runs_desc: Изпълнявайте прегледи безопасно, след което преминете към записи на живо, когато проектът е готов.
projectAi.run_health_desc: Сканиране само за четене за просрочена, блокирана и невъзложена работа.
projectAi.run_triage_desc: Опресни приоритета на задачите и добави етикети за триаж в целия бек лог.
projectAi.run_planning_desc: Подгответе следващата партида спринт, без да записвате в проекта.
projectAi.run_bulk_desc: Създайте планирани спринтове последователно и по избор предварително възложете задачи от бек лога.
projectAi.live_runtime_desc: Състояние и логове в реално време за изпълненията, които се случват в този проект в момента.
projectAi.no_active_runs: В момента няма активни изпълнения. Започнете преглед, за да видите как напредъкът и логовете се появяват тук.
projectAi.run_active_logs: Изпълнението е активно. Логовете ще се излъчват по-долу, докато агентът работи.
projectAi.recent_runs_desc: Най-скорошните изпълнения на агенти за този проект, включително прегледи и записи на живо.
projectAi.no_runs: Все още няма изпълнения на агенти. Започнете със сканиране на състоянието на проекта или преглед на планирането на спринт.

hero.selfHostMeta: Бесплатно навсегда при self-hosting · Три контейнера: Postgres, Redis, один web-образ

footer.description: Разрабатывается открыто. Ваши задачи живут в вашем Postgres и уходят, когда вы скажете.
footer.trademarks: Jira, Linear и Plane являются товарными знаками их владельцев.

finalCta.description: Создайте workspace за минуты или запустите все на своем сервере сегодня вечером; в любом случае код открыт для вас.

proof.headline: Open source, самостоятельный хостинг, ваши данные остаются вашими.

selfHost.title: Your work. Your server. Your data.
selfHost.description: Three containers and one command. Everything lives in your Postgres, exporting is a pg_dump away, and nothing phones home — no vendor lock-in by design.

postgres.title: One Postgres, one web image
postgres.body: Postgres 16 with the pgvector extension powers search; Redis 7 is along for the ride. No Mongo, no Elastic, no sidecar zoo.

migrations.title: Migrations run on boot
migrations.body: The image applies every database migration at startup. Upgrading is pulling a newer tag and restarting — nothing manual.

health.title: Health you can probe
health.body: GET /api/health returns 200 when the app is ready — wire it to your load balancer, uptime checks, or compose healthcheck.

pin.title: Pin the version you trust
pin.body: Set TASKNEBULA_IMAGE=neuraparse/tasknebula:0.4.0 to lock a release, or follow :latest for the bleeding edge.

selfHost.terminalTitle: quickstart — Docker Desktop or any Docker host (~2 min)
selfHost.terminalNote: The web image waits for Postgres, runs migrations, then serves on http://localhost:3000 — open it and finish the first-run admin wizard. Image: neuraparse/tasknebula.

migrate.title: Move from Jira or Linear in an afternoon
migrate.description: Switching tools shouldn't mean losing your history or your process. Import what you have, map it to TaskNebula, and keep working the way your team already does.

issues.title: Bring your issues over
issues.body: Import issues from Jira and Linear with their descriptions, comments, and history intact — not flattened into a CSV dump.

labels.title: Labels and sprints come too
labels.body: Labels, components, and active sprints land as first-class records, so your boards and backlog look familiar on day one.

fields.title: Map fields, not just names
fields.body: Match statuses, priorities, and custom fields to TaskNebula equivalents during import — review the mapping before anything writes.

workflow.title: Keep your workflow
workflow.body: Your statuses, swimlanes, and conventions move with you. Switch tools without retraining the team or rebuilding process from scratch.

migrate.note: Imports run against your own database and are fully reversible — nothing leaves your server, and you can re-run a mapping until it looks right.

aiMcp.title: Agents work the backlog with you.
aiMcp.description: TaskNebula was built for the agent era. Ask questions across your whole workspace, let AI triage and estimate new work, and give external agents real tools through a first-class MCP server.

ask.title: Ask your workspace
ask.body: The /api/ask RAG endpoint answers in natural language over your issues, docs, and comments — grounded in pgvector retrieval, with citations back to the source.

triage.title: AI triage & estimates
triage.body: Suggested priority, labels, and effort land on new issues — backed by real OpenAI and Anthropic calls, a cost guard, and tracing. You stay in control: accept or dismiss.

mcp.title: MCP tools for agents
mcp.body: @tasknebula/mcp-server exposes the backlog over MCP — HTTP at /api/mcp or stdio — so external agents create, search, and move issues with revocable keys.

workflow.title: One thread from intake to shipped.
workflow.description: No tool-hopping between a tracker, a planning doc, and an agent console. The work moves through a single surface — humans and agents on the same timeline.

intake.title: Intake without the noise
intake.body: Issues, requests, and inbound from your agents land in one queue. Triage suggests priority and labels so the backlog starts structured, not as a pile.

plan.title: Plan the sprint in place
plan.body: Pull from the backlog into a sprint, set fix versions and components, and read scope at a glance. Planning lives next to the work, not in a separate doc.

build.title: Build alongside agents
build.body: Agents are first-class teammates over MCP — they pick up issues, comment, and open work the same way people do, with every action on the shared timeline.

ship.title: Ship and track the outcome
ship.body: Close issues with a real resolution, cut the version, and watch velocity and cycle time update live. Done means done — not a column you forgot to clear.

comparison.title: TaskNebula vs Jira vs Linear vs Plane
comparison.description: All four are capable tools. Here is exactly where TaskNebula differs — open by default, yours to host, AI-native. Nothing more, nothing less.
comparison.tableCaption: Feature comparison between TaskNebula, Jira, Linear, and Plane. Each cell is marked Yes, Partial, or No, followed by a short detail.
comparison.footnote: Vendor details reflect public documentation and pricing pages as of June 2026 and may change. Jira is a trademark of Atlassian, Linear of Linear Orbit, and Plane of Plane Software — all are solid products, none affiliated with TaskNebula.

faq.title: Fair questions, straight answers.
faq.description: No sales gate, no asterisks. If yours is missing, open a GitHub issue — public answers beat private threads.
faq.statusPrompt: Still unsure? <link>Ask on GitHub</link> or read the live status in the docs.

freeMit.question: Is TaskNebula really free, and really MIT?
freeMit.answer: Yes — the whole app is MIT licensed: boards, sprints, docs, analytics, the AI surfaces, and the MCP server. No open-core split, no paywalled tier, no per-seat fee. Self-host it and run it forever for unlimited users; fork it if you want to.

selfHost.question: Can I self-host it?
selfHost.answer: That is the default. Bring Docker and you get three containers — Postgres 16 with pgvector, Redis 7, and the neuraparse/tasknebula web image. Migrations run on boot, GET /api/health is your probe endpoint, and a one-line compose quickstart gets you to localhost:3000. There is no hosted-only feature you lose by running it yourself.

aiKeys.question: Do I need the AI features? Can I bring my own keys?
aiKeys.answer: AI is optional — every board, sprint, and doc works with no model keys configured. When you want it, you bring your own OpenAI or Anthropic key as environment variables, so spend and data residency stay yours. There is a cost guard so a runaway prompt cannot run up your bill.

aiReality.question: How real are the AI features today?
aiReality.answer: We build them in the open. The data model and endpoints — triage suggestions, pgvector semantic search, and the /api/ask RAG endpoint — are live, and they make real OpenAI and Anthropic calls. Some surfaces are still labeled preview while we harden them; we would rather say that here than have you discover it after install.

mcpServer.question: What is the MCP server?
mcpServer.answer: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server exposes TaskNebula as tools — create, search, move, and comment on issues — so agents like Claude Code and Cursor can drive your tracker. It ships with the repo, HTTP at /api/mcp or stdio via @tasknebula/mcp-server, and is MIT licensed. Heads up: end-to-end agent auth depends on the REST API accepting API keys, which is still being wired, so treat it as preview today.

imports.question: Does it import from Jira or Linear?
imports.answer: Yes — there are importers for Jira, Linear, GitHub, and CSV. Each runs with a preview step so you can inspect the field mapping before anything is written, and you can keep your old tool read-only while you cut over.

productionReady.question: Is it production-ready?
productionReady.answer: The core tracker — issues, boards, sprints, docs, search, permissions, REST API, and OpenAPI spec — is solid and self-hostable. Some edges are still maturing: OAuth login needs a DB adapter, so use credentials auth for now; realtime collab env vars are not yet plumbed into the Docker images; and parts of the AI/MCP surface are preview. We track all of it openly in docs/STATUS.md.

dataVisibility.question: Who can see my data?
dataVisibility.answer: You decide. Self-hosted, everything lives in your Postgres on your infrastructure and exports with a plain pg_dump — no telemetry phones home. Inside the product, workspaces are isolated per organization with role-based access, permission schemes, and audit logs.

features.title: From keystroke to ship — one open backlog.
features.description: Plan, execute, and reason over the work in the same surface. Keyboard-first delivery, an AI layer you can audit, and agents that move tickets through a real MCP server — all MIT-licensed and self-hostable.

keyboard.title: Keyboard-first boards and sprints
keyboard.body: Drive the whole board from one Cmd+K palette — search, navigate, and run actions without leaving the keys. Drag across columns, plan sprints, and read burndown in the same view.

import.title: Import from Jira and Linear
import.body: Bring your backlog with you — Jira, Linear, and CSV importers map issues, statuses, and metadata so you start full, not empty.

structure.title: Jira-parity structure
structure.body: First-class labels, components, fix versions, and a resolution model — backlog structure stored in real tables and REST APIs, not JSON strings.

ai.title: AI-native, auditable
ai.body: Real OpenAI and Anthropic calls behind a cost guard and tracing — triage suggestions, AI estimates, and a /api/ask RAG endpoint over pgvector. No black boxes.

agents.title: Agents work the backlog
agents.body: A real MCP server — HTTP at /api/mcp or stdio — lets coding agents create, search, and move issues. Backed by a REST API, OpenAPI spec, revocable keys, and webhooks.

realtime.title: Realtime collaboration
realtime.body: Specs and issue docs edit live — Tiptap on Yjs with presence and conflict-free merges, so two people on one doc just works.

selfHost.title: Self-host in minutes
selfHost.body: One Docker image, Postgres, and you own the data. Multi-tenant from the schema up — every table is organization-scoped, no vendor lock-in.

analytics.title: Analytics close to execution
analytics.body: Velocity, cycle time, and priority mix per project — with a resolution model that separates Done from Won't Fix and Duplicate.

board.title: A board you can actually move through.
board.description: Switch projects, drag issues between stages, and keep details close without fake motion or noisy chrome.

voice.joinSetupSubtitle: Изберете микрофон веднъж преди свързване. Това прави самостоятелно хостваните обаждания по-стабилни.

voice.exactSelectionReliable: Точният избор на микрофон става надежден, след като браузърът предостави достъп до микрофона.
voice.labelsNotExposed: Браузърът все още не е разкрил етикетите на микрофоните; опресняването след връщане от настройките на браузъра обикновено решава това.
voice.exactSwitchingReliable: Точното превключване на устройства става надеждно, след като се предостави достъп на браузъра до микрофона.
voice.preparingRoom: Подготовка на токена на стаята и състоянието на обаждането…

voice.joinHintGranted: Достъпът на браузъра вече е разрешен, така че TaskNebula ще се опита да запази избрания от вас микрофон по време на присъединяването.
voice.joinHintNotGranted: Преди да се предостави достъп на браузъра, Chromium и Safari може да преминат към системния микрофон по подразбиране или устройството, избрано в подканата на браузъра.
voice.joinHintFirefox: Firefox обикновено може да запази избрания микрофон през целия процес на присъединяване.
voice.selfMonitorBlocked: Възпроизвеждането за самостоятелно наблюдение беше блокирано от браузъра. Опитайте превключвателя отново, ако е необходимо.
voice.audioContextUnavailable: AudioContext е недостъпен в този браузър.
voice.audioContextFailed: AudioContext не можа да стартира с текущото аудио устройство.
voice.browserStabilityFallback: Chromium и Safari сега стартират първо със системния микрофон по подразбиране за по-бързо и надеждно присъединяване. Можете да смените входовете, след като стаята се свърже.
voice.switchedToDefault: Избраният микрофон не можа да стартира, така че TaskNebula премина към системния ви микрофон по подразбиране.
voice.micSwitchedToDefault: Избраният микрофон не можа да стартира в този браузър, така че TaskNebula премина към системния ви микрофон по подразбиране.
voice.joinedMutedFallback: {error} Присъединихте се със заглушен звук, за да може обаждането да започне веднага. Можете да включите микрофона, след като сте вътре.
voice.enableAudioHint: активирайте звука веднъж, ако възпроизвеждането е блокирано
voice.speakerStartFailed: Изходът към високоговорителя не можа да стартира на това устройство.

voice.micCouldNotStartBusy: Микрофонът ви не можа да бъде стартиран. Възможно е друго приложение да го използва.
voice.audioEngineFailed: Аудио ядрото на браузъра отказа за това устройство. Спрете другите аудио приложения или сменете микрофона си, след което опитайте отново.
voice.noMicForSession: Не беше намерен микрофон за тази сесия на браузъра.

microphoneRecoveryHint.chromium: Look for the microphone prompt in the address bar and choose Allow this time or Allow on every visit. If it was dismissed, open Chrome site settings for this site and allow the microphone.
microphoneRecoveryHint.edge: Look for the microphone prompt in the address bar. If it was dismissed, open Edge Site permissions for this site and allow the microphone.
microphoneRecoveryHint.firefox: Firefox usually shows microphone access near the left side of the address bar. If it disappeared, open the padlock or Page Info permissions and allow the microphone for this site.
microphoneRecoveryHint.safari: Safari may keep microphone access under Safari > Settings > Websites > Microphone, or in the website controls in the address bar.
microphoneRecoveryHint.unknown: Check the browser microphone prompt or this site's permissions and allow microphone access.

voice.timedOutMicrophoneAccess: Microphone access timed out while waiting for the browser prompt. {hint} Then try the mic button again.
voice.timedOutPendingMicrophonePrompt: Microphone access timed out while waiting for the browser prompt. {hint} If you approve it now, TaskNebula will still turn your mic on automatically. If no prompt appears, refresh this page or try the mic button again.
voice.microphoneCaptureUnsupported: This browser does not support microphone capture.
voice.microphoneNotFound: No microphone was found. Check your audio input device and try again.
voice.microphoneBusy: Your microphone is busy in another app. Close other audio apps and try again.

permissionHelp.grantedNoDevices: Microphone access is allowed, but no audio input devices are currently visible to the browser.
permissionHelp.denied: Microphone access is blocked for this site. {hint}
permissionHelp.prompt: This browser is still waiting for a microphone decision. {hint}{labelsHiddenNote}
permissionHelp.unknown: This browser does not expose a reliable microphone permission state before access. {hint}{labelsHiddenNote}

aiModelCards.disclosureBadge: Разкриване по член 50 от EU AI Act
aiModelCards.title: Карти на AI моделите
aiModelCards.intro: TaskNebula предоставя на крайните потребители следните AI функции. За всяка от тях публикуваме предназначението, идентичността на модела, данните, изпращани при всяко обръщение, политиката за съхранение и нашата подразбираща се позиция за човешки надзор. Тази страница е публичен запис за прозрачност и важи за всички внедрявания на TaskNebula. Версия на разкриването <version></version>.
aiModelCards.lastReviewed: Последно прегледано на 2026-05-14. Прилагането на регламента започва на 2026-08-02.
aiModelCards.footerContact: Въпроси относно прозрачността на AI, правата на субектите на данни или тези карти на модели? Пишете на <link></link>.
